source_name: kWh Analytics — Solar Risk Assessment 2026 landing page
solar_hail_use: Open page exposes the solar-hail article title and the modeled pass/fail definition; full report values should be requested/downloaded.
has_open_numbers: Yes: 100 MW reference project, 1-in-100 hail loss, 10% asset-value fail threshold, 13% of U.S. headline.
does_not_have_open: Full geographic result table, by-state/county bands, all module/stow configuration results, underlying modeled AAL/PML values.
notes: Full report download is form-gated; public page still contains the most important benchmark-threshold definition for solar hail.

solar_hail_use: Open portfolio-loss pattern: hail dominates financial losses despite low incident count; useful for shape and peril-share sanity.
has_open_numbers: 73% of total financial losses by damage amount; 6% of loss incidents; 19% of hail-related losses in North Carolina; 23% sites with at least one partial loss in kWh database; 29% of loss sites with multiple events.
does_not_have_open: Project-level loss ledger; denominator/TIV for each record; AAL/PML by location.
notes: Very strong source for peril-share / frequency-vs-severity pattern; not a direct AAL table.

source_name: kWh Analytics / POWER Magazine — Examining the Real Cost of Renewable Resiliency
solar_hail_use: Open modeled AAL for standard vs resilient 100-MW solar project in high hail-risk region.
has_open_numbers: 100-MW project; standard design net loss AAL $1,062,720; resilient design net loss AAL $307,790; 71% AAL reduction; standard cost $100M, resilient $115M; deductible noted as 5% of property damage value at risk with min/max terms.
does_not_have_open: Exact site, detailed stochastic distribution, gross vs net bridge, full model assumptions.
notes: Excellent numeric AAL anchor, but it is modeled net loss and a case study, not a ground-up claims ledger.

source_name: kWh Analytics / Nextpower — hail-ready solar telematics program
solar_hail_use: Pathway to real stow-performance data and insurer-modeled AAL/premium deltas for advanced stow assets.
has_open_numbers: Open statement that resilient configurations materially reduce portfolio AAL and premiums; 2024 case reference that 75° stow reduces damage probability 87% vs 60°; kWh database size 300,000+ zero-carbon projects and $150B loss data.
does_not_have_open: Project-level stow compliance logs, loss runs, premium deltas by asset, AAL tables.
notes: Should be treated as an acquisition pathway rather than a published benchmark set.

source_name: AXIS Global Energy — Solar vs. Hail / severe weather claims report
solar_hail_use: Best open claim-severity source found for solar hail; directly gives $/MW severity by stow/fixed-tilt/module-glass state.
has_open_numbers: Hail = 55% of total gross NatCat/extreme-weather solar PV claim amount in U.S./Canada; global hail second most costly after strong wind; average claim severity $150k/MW successful stow, $340k/MW fixed tilt, $380k/MW failed tracker stow; heat-strengthened glass +$50k/MW vs fully tempered; NatCat/weather downtime 240 days average.
does_not_have_open: Full claim distribution; project TIV; location-level claim table; deductible/coverage terms.
notes: Most immediately actionable open source for $/MW event-severity bands.

source_name: Risk & Insurance / Gallagher Re / AXIS — rising hail risk and solar insurability
solar_hail_use: Adds global closed-claim context: module count, capacity, gross claims and geographic rank.
has_open_numbers: Closed solar PV claims 2019–2025: hail 27% of NatCat/extreme-weather losses globally by total claim amount, >1M modules damaged, $342M accumulated gross claims; Texas gross claim amount order-of-magnitude greater than Nebraska; heat-strengthened glass +$50k/MW.
does_not_have_open: Full loss run; per-project TIV; event-level capacity affected; denominators needed to avoid over-normalization.
notes: Useful corroboration and global scale; less precise than the AXIS official $/MW severity bullets.

source_name: GCube / Tokio Marine HCC — Hail No! public summaries
solar_hail_use: Open public summaries provide claim-count vs incurred-cost asymmetry and average claim size.
has_open_numbers: Hail = about 54% of incurred costs of total solar losses while only 1.4% of claims; average hail claim about $58.4M; GCube insures >100 GW renewables in 40 countries.
does_not_have_open: Capacity denominator per claim; TIV; project geography; full Hail No report details may require subscription/contact.
notes: The $58.4M per-claim figure is powerful but must be capacity-normalized with care.

source_name: VDE Americas — Hail Risk Intelligence / PML-AAL / PRE / Hail Atlas
solar_hail_use: Best gated path to comparison-ready AAL/PML and return-period hail loss numbers for solar PV.
has_open_numbers: Product description exposes exact requested metrics: PML, AAL, downside PML, BI, return-interval curves, fixed-return-period hail size, multiple tracker tilts, sample outcome distributions, P50/P90/P95 cumulative losses, 1MW/100MW atlas scaling, portfolio PML/AAL and correlated PRE.
does_not_have_open: Actual project/county AAL and PML values unless report/subscription is acquired.
notes: This is the cleanest source pathway for future financial-range comparison, but not open-data.

source_name: VDE Americas / Cirrus Advisers / GRC — all-natural catastrophe risk and insurability reports
solar_hail_use: Pathway for project-specific, technology-specific hail PML/AAL and broader NatCat comparison context.
has_open_numbers: Open pages confirm site- and technology-specific financial loss/risk estimates based on lat/long and PV module/tracker technologies.
does_not_have_open: Actual report values; requires transaction/client report or commercial access.
notes: Especially relevant for lender/insurance diligence contexts.

source_name: VDE Americas — 2025 SRA hail stow case summaries
solar_hail_use: Open stow-performance case evidence: hail sizes, stow angles, damage/no-damage outcomes.
has_open_numbers: Radar-estimated hail 40–50mm, 50–75mm, 75–100mm; all projects had 2.0mm/2.0mm dual glass; 52° stow; two no-damage outcomes and one minor-damage outcome tied to unstowed rows / motor issue.
notes: Useful for sanity checking stow-conditioner behavior and damage caps, not a financial benchmark.

source_name: Renew Risk — U.S. severe convective storm model for utility-scale solar
solar_hail_use: Gated/partner path to cat-model solar SCS loss outputs including hail, tornado, and wind.
has_open_numbers: Open summaries describe model scope, hail/tornado/wind coverage, glass thickness, stow angle, mechanical reliability, exposure database updated monthly with TIV and BI costs.
does_not_have_open: Actual loss tables, event sets, AAL/PML or county bands.
notes: Potentially very valuable if access is available; current open info is pathway-only.

source_name: J.S. Held — hail damage to solar arrays and claims/case-study context
solar_hail_use: Useful named-event/event-range and forensic pathway, including report-bias warnings.
has_open_numbers: Midway Solar loss cited around $70–80M; annual hail claims $5M–$80M; 2022 renewable hail damages $300–400M; Pecos County hail-report undercount example.
does_not_have_open: Clean project capacity/TIV for each claim; formal loss-run distribution; coverage terms.
notes: Good for cap/event-severity context; event denominators should be researched before converting to $/MW.

source_name: DOE / FEMP — Hail Damage Mitigation for PV Systems
solar_hail_use: Mechanism and threshold context for PV hail damage; not a financial benchmark.
has_open_numbers: Very large hail >1.75 in / 44mm can cause significant module damage; severe hail described as greatest contributor to insured losses on solar PV systems worldwide.
notes: Useful for damage-curve mechanism and stow protocol validation, not financial-range benchmarking.

solar_hail_use: Exposure denominator for CONUS/state/county solar-hail aggregation and asset-location joins.
has_open_numbers: v4.0/April 2026; 6,611 facilities; 49 states plus DC; latest operation Q4 2025; front-of-meter PV facilities with direct-current capacity >=1 MW; array boundaries/locations and attributes.
does_not_have_open: Losses, claims, insured values, resilience configuration for every asset.
notes: Not a benchmark by itself; required for normalizing external claims and model outputs.

solar_hail_use: Generator-level metadata and capacity/status crosswalk; useful with USPVDB for exposure reconciliation.
has_open_numbers: Form EIA-860 collects generator-level data for existing/planned generators at plants with >=1 MW combined nameplate capacity; 2025 early release posted June 9, 2026.
notes: Critical for capacity/vintage/owner status; no direct loss numbers.
